sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀναπτύσσω

- Transliteration: Anaptusso

- Phonetic: an-ap-toos'-o

- Definition:

1. to unroll

a. the books of the Hebrews were rolls fastened to [one or] two smooth rods and furnished with handles, so that they could be rolled up and unrolled

- Origin: from G303 (in the sense of reversal) and G4428

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G303 (in the sense of reversal) and G4428; to unroll (a scroll or volume): - open.
